name: Peking Raviolis
recipeIngredient: 1 package eggroll wrappers or ravioli wrappers
1/4 cup soy sauce or tamari sauce
recipeInstructions:
This weekend we were snowed in, which led to a craving for Chinese food. I bought eggroll wrappers and made Peking Raviolis with Eggplant.
1. Slice the eggplant lengthwise and remove stems. Place face-down on a cookie sheet. Use non-stick spray if needed.
2. Bake for about 1 hour until tender. Remove from oven and let cool.
3. While cooling, mix remaining ingredients in a bowl to make a marinade.
4. When eggplant is cool and slightly shriveled, scoop out insides into the marinade. Let sit for at least 15 minutes.
5. Puree eggplant and marinade in a food processor for 30 seconds.
6. If using wonton wrappers or ravioli wrappers, skip this step. Cut two eggroll wrappers into even quarters.
7. Place about 8 wrapper pieces on a flat surface. Spoon half teaspoon of eggplant puree onto the center of each wrapper.
8. Dip fingers in water and seal edges by folding over to form a triangle. Place on a rack to dry.
9. Repeat with remaining wrappers.
10. If too much filling is in a wrapper, press firmly to ensure sealing.
11. Bring water to a rolling boil. Stir circularly with a slotted spoon, lower one ravioli at a time into the water.
12. Continue stirring until raviolis float and are slightly transparent. Remove with slotted spoon and place on drying rack.
13. Connect corners with a toothpick and serve on lettuce or ornamental cabbage.
